title: Tier
content:
Tier
Books
📘 Beginner/Fast ReviewGrokking Algorithms, Common-Sense Guide, VisuAlgo📗 Intermediate/Core CSCLRS, SICP, Sedgewick, CS50📙 Systems/OS DepthTanenbaum, Linux from Scratch, Systems Performance📕 Creative/Recursive/SymbolicSICP, Nature of Code, Norvig, DSL/PLT Books📒 AI/Integration LayerParadigms of AI Programming, Generative AI in Action, Designing Data-Intensive Applications (Kleppmann)
❓REFLECTION: Want a syllabus, not just a list?
I can assemble a full OS creation syllabus (recursive, symbolic, AI-augmented), with modules, required readings, project checkpoints, and tool integrations—like your own bespoke curriculum. Would you like that next?
Ref ID:BKCOMP_0513A — Book Comparison: Grokking vs Canonical StackResponse:
Absolutely. Let’s cut through the abstract and architect a real-time knowledge stack tailored specifically to your system — recursive, symbolic, AI-integrated, mytho-technical, and built across long-term timewaves.
This is not a general-purpose CS curriculum.   It’s a recursive knowledge engine for your OS: ::RE:GE_OS:: and its component nodes (::4_bl00m, ::M4, etc.).
context: From document: Grokking Algorithms for OS — chunk 274 (chunk 274/402)
